source_pr.title: executor: inject random panic to AggExec
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #xxx <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: 
In the project Tracking_Aggregate_Memory, there are many tracker.Consume, that will trigger panic when memory usage is too large. We should keep TiDB can cancel the sql successfully, and doesn't leak some resource.

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed: 

How it Works:

### Related changes


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM
- Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note <!-- Please write a release note here to describe the change you made when it is released to the users of TiDB. If your PR doesn't involve any change to TiDB(like test enhancements, RFC proposals...), you can write `No release note`. -->

symptoms: Intermittent hang / race-prone behavior in aggregation executor tests when injecting random OOM panics (AggExec parallel hash agg) and the executor is not properly synchronized/closed
failure_signature: hang forever / race in executor/aggregate (HashAggExec parallel workers + finalOutputCh lifecycle)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency hazards in async execution/cleanup: HashAggExec runs multiple worker goroutines and sends results/errors to `finalOutputCh`. When random OOM panics were injected, some paths could hang or race because producers/consumers and channel close timing were not strictly synchronized, and tests also didn't always close the record set after consuming rows. This PR stabilizes it by ensuring record sets are closed in the test, increasing `finalOutputCh` buffering, and only closing `finalOutputCh` after all relevant goroutines (fetch child + partial workers + final workers) have exited.
fix_pattern: Ensure async worker goroutines are fully joined and output channels are closed only after all producers exit; close result sets in tests to stop background work and avoid hang/race conditions.
analysis:
PR title: executor: inject random panic to AggExec
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/23139

Triage summary: fix some race
Changed files (3): executor/adapter.go, executor/aggregate.go, executor/aggregate_test.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
